svg.title: Post-processing stack
svg.desc: Diagram showing forward and backward trajectories entering conservative merge resolution, relinking, interpolation, and final export.
svg.text: Trajectory Resolution Stack
The current system favors conservative identity continuity over aggressive gap filling
Forward Trajectories
Primary pass written during normal playback.
Best for causal continuity and GUI feedback.
Backward Trajectories
Optional reverse replay over the same cached detections.
Useful when crossings confuse the forward-only hypothesis.
Conservative Merge
Pairs are accepted only if enough overlapping frames agree
within `AGREEMENT_DISTANCE`. Disagreeing sections can split
rather than forcing a risky identity merge.
Redundant or spatially contained fragments are removed.
Fragment Stitching and Relinking
Short gaps are stitched when motion remains plausible.
A second relinking pass can also use pose prototypes to
reject motion-compatible but shape-incompatible joins.
This targets fragmented tracks after turns or occlusions.
Interpolation
Short missing spans are filled with linear, cubic, or
spline interpolation with explicit gap limits.
Analysis-Ready Output
Final CSV rows, confidence fields, optional pose columns,
and crop datasets for downstream behavioral analysis.
